skill.name: survival-analysis-km
skill.description: Kaplan-Meier survival analysis tool for clinical and biological research. Generates publication-ready survival curves with statistical tests.
skill.summary: Kaplan-Meier survival analysis tool for clinical and biological research. Generates publication-ready survival curves with statistical tests.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 83 static findings are FALSE POSITIVES. The 56 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' findings are markdown inline code formatting (backtick syntax for code spans and fenced code blocks), not shell command execution. The 12 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' findings are statistical formulas (product-limit estimator, Cox model notation), package names, and file paths - no actual cryptography. The 3 'Path traversal' findings are security checklist items explicitly warning against traversal ('no ../ traversal'). Network URL findings reference legitimate library documentation (lifelines, scikit-survival) and the skill's own GitHub repository. The os.makedirs call at scripts/main.py:392 is legitimate directory creation for analysis output. This is a legitimate survival analysis data science skill with transparent documentation and no malicious intent.

low_findings.title: Markdown backtick formatting misidentified as shell execution
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: 56 instances across SKILL.md, references/README.md, and references/runtime_checklist.md. The static analyzer flagged markdown inline code formatting (single backticks for code spans like 'scripts/main.py') and fenced code blocks (triple backticks for bash/csv/text) as Ruby shell backtick execution. All are standard Markdown syntax for code formatting, not shell command execution. No command injection or dynamic execution risk exists.

low_findings.title: Statistical notation misidentified as cryptographic algorithm
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: 12 instances across multiple files. The static analyzer flagged mathematical notation (product-limit estimator formula S(t), Cox proportional hazards model, Greenwood formula), statistical package names, file paths, and table formatting as 'weak cryptographic algorithm'. These are legitimate statistical formulas and content for a survival analysis skill. No cryptographic operations exist in the codebase.

low_findings.title: Security checklist references misidentified as path traversal
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: The static analyzer flagged security checklist items in SKILL.md that explicitly warn against path traversal. Line 224 states '[ ] No unauthorized file system access (../)' and line 227 states '[ ] Input file paths validated (no ../ traversal)'. These are security best-practice items documenting the skill's safety posture, not actual traversal attempts.

low_findings.title: Library documentation URLs misidentified as network risk
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: Hardcoded URLs in references/README.md point to lifelines.readthedocs.io and scikit-survival.readthedocs.io, which are legitimate open-source library documentation pages for survival analysis Python packages. SKILL.md contains the skill's GitHub source URL. No data exfiltration, credential leakage, or malicious network activity. The Python script scripts/main.py makes no HTTP requests.

low_findings.title: Normal documentation text misidentified as system reconnaissance
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: Lines flagged as 'system reconnaissance' contain standard documentation content: output discipline guidance (SKILL.md:60), audit-ready command descriptions (SKILL.md:72), and test case descriptions for invalid input handling (SKILL.md:251). These are routine developer documentation, not reconnaissance or information gathering.

low_findings.title: Legitimate filesystem operations for data analysis
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: os.makedirs at scripts/main.py:392 is a standard Python call to create the output directory for analysis results. The script reads a CSV input file (pd.read_csv), performs survival analysis, and writes output files (plots, CSV tables, text report) to the designated output directory. All filesystem operations are scoped to user-specified paths and are essential for the skill's documented data analysis functionality.

content.user_title: Perform Kaplan-Meier Survival Analysis with Statistical Tests
content.value_statement: Clinical researchers need reliable survival analysis to compare treatment groups and generate publication-ready results. This skill automates Kaplan-Meier curve generation, log-rank testing, and Cox proportional hazards regression from clinical datasets.
actual_capabilities: Generates Kaplan-Meier survival curves with 95% confidence intervals for up to 10 comparison groups
Performs log-rank, Wilcoxon, and Peto-Peto statistical tests for comparing survival distributions between groups
Calculates hazard ratios with 95% confidence intervals using Cox proportional hazards regression
Produces publication-ready plots in PNG and PDF formats at configurable resolution
Computes median survival times, censoring rates, and restricted mean survival time (RMST)
Generates structured summary reports with all statistical findings for review
limitations: Does not handle time-dependent covariates or competing risks scenarios
Requires event data in strict binary format (0 = censored, 1 = event)
Statistical validity depends on proportional hazards assumption being met
Results should be reviewed by a qualified biostatistician before publication

use_cases.title: Clinical Trial Outcome Analysis
use_cases.description: Compare survival outcomes between treatment and control groups in a randomized clinical trial. Generate log-rank test results and hazard ratios for regulatory submission.

use_cases.title: Academic Publication Preparation
use_cases.description: Produce publication-ready Kaplan-Meier curves and statistical summaries for cancer research manuscripts and conference presentations.

use_cases.title: Medical Data Exploration
use_cases.description: Explore patient survival patterns across multiple treatment cohorts to identify promising approaches and generate hypotheses for further study.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Survival Curve
prompt_templates.prompt: Run survival analysis on the clinical dataset at data.csv. Use time_months for the time column and death for the event column. Generate a Kaplan-Meier curve and save results to ./output/.
prompt_templates.scenario: First-time user with a single-group survival dataset

prompt_templates.title: Treatment Group Comparison
prompt_templates.prompt: Perform survival analysis comparing treatment groups. Input file: trial_data.csv. Time column: overall_survival. Event column: deceased. Group column: treatment_arm. Output directory: ./results/. Include log-rank test results.
prompt_templates.scenario: Researcher comparing two or more treatment groups

prompt_templates.title: Publication-Ready Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate publication-quality survival analysis from patient_data.csv. Use survival_months as time, event_occurred as event, and cohort as group. Output to ./publication/. Include risk table, 300 DPI figures, and Cox regression hazard ratios.
prompt_templates.scenario: Researcher preparing figures and tables for journal submission

prompt_templates.title: Comprehensive Multi-Group Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Run full survival analysis pipeline on study_data.csv. Time column: follow_up_months. Event column: mortality. Group column: therapy_type. Output to ./full_analysis/. Enable risk table, set confidence level to 0.99, include pairwise log-rank comparisons between all groups, generate Cox regression with concordance index, and create a complete summary report.
prompt_templates.scenario: Senior biostatistician performing comprehensive multi-cohort analysis

output_examples.input: Run survival analysis comparing Drug A and Placebo groups from clinical_trial.csv
output_examples.output: The analysis generated a Kaplan-Meier survival curve showing Drug A has significantly better survival than Placebo (log-rank p = 0.0023). Median survival for Drug A was 28.4 months (95% CI: 24.1-32.7) compared to 18.2 months (95% CI: 15.3-21.1) for Placebo. The hazard ratio was 0.62 (95% CI: 0.45-0.85), indicating a 38% risk reduction. Output files include km_curve.png, survival_stats.csv, logrank_test.csv, and hazard_ratios.csv in the output directory.

output_examples.input: Generate a Kaplan-Meier curve with risk table for the oncology trial dataset
output_examples.output: The survival curve shows three treatment arms with distinct survival trajectories. The risk table below the x-axis displays the number of patients remaining at risk at each 6-month interval. Arm A maintains the highest survival probability throughout follow-up, while Arm C shows the steepest early decline. All confidence bands are displayed as shaded regions around each curve.

best_practices: Validate that your event column contains only binary values (0 for censored, 1 for event) before running the analysis
Check the proportional hazards assumption using Schoenfeld residuals when reporting Cox regression results
Review the censoring rate before interpreting results - rates above 50% may reduce reliability of median survival estimates
anti_patterns: Running survival analysis on datasets with fewer than 30 events per group, which produces unreliable statistical estimates
Using Kaplan-Meier analysis for competing risks scenarios where cumulative incidence functions would be more appropriate
Interpreting hazard ratios without verifying the proportional hazards assumption using Schoenfeld residuals

faq.question: What file formats does the skill support for input data?
faq.answer: The skill accepts CSV files with columns for time, event indicator, and optional grouping variable.

faq.question: What is a survival curve and how do I read it?
faq.answer: A survival curve shows the probability of surviving beyond each time point. The y-axis shows survival probability from 0 to 1, and the x-axis shows time. A steeper drop means more events occurred in that period.

faq.question: What statistical tests does this skill perform?
faq.answer: The skill performs log-rank tests for comparing survival curves between groups and Cox proportional hazards regression for calculating hazard ratios with confidence intervals.

faq.question: Can I use this skill for non-medical survival analysis?
faq.answer: Yes, Kaplan-Meier analysis applies to any time-to-event data, including engineering reliability, customer churn analysis, and social science studies.

faq.question: What is the difference between a log-rank test and a hazard ratio?
faq.answer: The log-rank test assesses whether survival curves differ significantly between groups. The hazard ratio quantifies the magnitude of that difference as an instantaneous relative risk.

faq.question: How do I interpret the confidence intervals on the survival curve?
faq.answer: The shaded bands represent the 95% confidence interval for the survival probability at each time point. Wider bands indicate greater uncertainty, typically at later time points with fewer patients at risk.
